refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; minimize teleoperation latency via low-latency network link; design safety services with non-blocking async checks.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time adaptive control to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under varying site conditions.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; enforce budget via design reviews and integration testing.
DP-D: Establish telemetry-driven feedback loop to continuously monitor p95 cycle time and trigger corrective actions when threshold is approached.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Allocate explicit cycle time budget across vehicle (load/travel/dump mechanics), teleoperation (network latency + control overhead), and safety services (non-blocking checks). Establish continuous telemetry monitoring of p95 cycle time with feedback-driven corrective actions. This approach is traceable, measurable, and supports safety-critical design by making trade-offs explicit.

potential_sources: Vehicle subsystem cycle time is coupled to hydraulic pressure, load mass, and terrain (site conditions); teleoperation latency is coupled to network infrastructure (Network Link interface); safety service latency is coupled to sensor response time and decision logic.
Coupling is manageable via explicit budget allocation and interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).
coupling_check.notes: No circular dependencies detected. DP-C allocates budgets; DP-D monitors and feeds back. Safety criticality is addressed by ensuring safety services do not block cycle time path (async design).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and network latency constraints.
Risk: If site conditions (terrain, load variability) exceed design envelope, p95 may not meet CT_target despite optimization.
Risk: Network latency spikes (congestion, interference) could violate teleoperation budget; mitigation requires network QoS guarantees.
Risk: Safety service blocking (e.g., emergency stop validation) could introduce unplanned delays; mitigation requires async/non-blocking architecture.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly state cycle time budget allocation percentages (e.g., vehicle 60%, teleoperation 25%, safety 15%) and define budget enforcement gates in design reviews. Refine DP-D to specify telemetry sampling rate, p95 computation window, and corrective action triggers.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for coordinating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and corrective action orchestration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end timestamps; computed p95 cycle time) to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle task definitions (load mass, travel distance, site conditions) from Site Manager or planning system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services and may emit corrective actions (e.g., reduce speed if safety margin eroding).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution manages network connectivity for teleoperation and telemetry; monitors latency and QoS.

module_containment_scores.notes: Cycle time KPI is a system-level concern requiring coordination across all subsystems. NGHE Solution is the natural owner of budget allocation, monitoring, and corrective action. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry emission and tasking reception cross boundaries.

Vehicle owns load/travel/dump mechanics and contributes majority of cycle time. However, cycle time KPI also depends on teleoperation latency and safety service delays, which are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning to Vehicle alone would create diffusion across teleoperation and safety modules.

Teleoperation owns network latency and control overhead, which are cycle time contributors. However, does not own vehicle mechanics or safety services. Assigning to Teleoperation alone would create high diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety-critical checks that may impact cycle time. However, does not own vehicle mechanics or teleoperation. Assigning to Safety Services alone would create high diffusion and risk safety-performance trade-off conflicts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that cannot be owned by a single subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services) without creating diffusion and accountability gaps. NGHE Solution, as the top-level integration module, is the appropriate owner. It coordinates budget allocation, monitors telemetry, and orchestrates corrective actions across subsystems.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it attempts to own detailed cycle time optimization logic for each subsystem. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns budget allocation and monitoring; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective optimization within allocated budgets.

Risk: Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient granularity (e.g., per-subsystem cycle time breakdown) to diagnose root causes of p95 violations. Mitigation: Enhance Telemetry interface to include subsystem-level timing data.
Risk: Corrective action feedback loop (DP-D) may introduce latency or complexity. Mitigation: Define corrective action triggers and response times in design; validate via simulation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify NGHE Solution ownership boundaries: (1) owns cycle time budget allocation and enforcement gates; (2) owns telemetry aggregation and p95 computation; (3) does NOT own detailed subsystem optimization (delegated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Define explicit interfaces for subsystem cycle time reporting (extend Telemetry interface with subsystem-level metrics).

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR clearly specifies measurable cycle time target (p95 ≤ CT_target) and monitoring requirement. Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry feedback) directly addresses FR by enabling measurement, allocation, and corrective action. DP is traceable to FR and supports high safety criticality via explicit non-blocking design principle.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Cycle time optimization logic may be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns budget allocation and monitoring; subsystems own optimization within budgets.
Diffusion risk: Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient subsystem-level granularity. Mitigation: Extend Telemetry interface specification to include per-subsystem cycle time metrics.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. Containment score 95 reflects that cycle time is a system-level emergent property requiring top-level coordination. Diffusion score 5 reflects minimal cross-boundary dependencies (Telemetry out, Tasking in, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link inout).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials with telemetry logging. p95 cycle time can be computed from logged timestamps. Safety event correlation can be analyzed to ensure no safety compromise. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) is measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Sub-allocations to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are defined in sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through 002.4). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify cycle time budget allocation percentages and budget enforcement gates in design reviews.
Refine DP-D to specify telemetry sampling rate, p95 computation window (e.g., rolling 1-hour window), and corrective action triggers.
Extend Telemetry interface specification to include per-subsystem cycle time metrics (vehicle load/travel/dump times, teleoperation latency, safety service decision time).
Define explicit non-blocking design principle for Safety Services to ensure safety checks do not introduce blocking delays in cycle time path.

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-002 is well-defined and traceable to DP and IM. However, the requirement is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through 002.4) that address vehicle mechanics, teleoperation latency, safety service design, and telemetry monitoring. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define detailed design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to next level is necessary to allocate cycle time budgets, define subsystem-level requirements, and establish verification strategy.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and network latency constraints.
Site conditions (terrain, load variability) remain within design envelope.
Network infrastructure supports required QoS for teleoperation latency.
Safety services can be designed with non-blocking async architecture.
Telemetry interface provides sufficient granularity for root cause diagnosis.
open_risks: Site conditions may exceed design envelope, making CT_target unachievable.
Network latency spikes could violate teleoperation budget.
Safety service blocking could introduce unplanned delays.
Telemetry interface may lack subsystem-level granularity for diagnosis.
Corrective action feedback loop may introduce latency or complexity.
evidence_needed: Cycle time budget allocation percentages (vehicle, teleoperation, safety) with justification.
Network QoS requirements and latency guarantees.
Telemetry interface specification with subsystem-level metrics.
Corrective action trigger definitions and response time requirements.
Field trial data validating p95 ≤ CT_target under representative duty cycle.
